subparts.text: What is wealth maximization? How is it superior to profit maximization? 1
Is there a similarity between the MM hypothesis and the net operating income approach? 2
An investment would cost `100,000 and provide annual cash inflow of 21,150 for 6 years. If the opportunity cost of capital is 10 per cent, what is the investment’s NPV? 3
‘Debt is the cheapest source of funds.’ Explain 2
What are the reasons for holding inventory? 4
What is meant by time value of money? What are the reasons for TVM? 1
Define payout ratio, retention ratio and dividend yield 3
Explain the concept of working capital. Are gross and net concepts of working capital exclusive? Discuss 4

questions.question_text: Investment, Financing and Dividend decision are all interrelated. Comment 1

i) Calculate the present value of ₹ 600 received one year from now; received at the end of five years; received at the end of fifteen years. Assume a 5 per cent time preference rate. Determine the present value of ₹700 each paid at the end of each of the next six years. Assume an 8 per cent of interest. Assuming a 10 per cent discount rate, compute the present value of ₹1,100; ₹900; ₹1,500 and ₹700 received at the end of one through four years. For calculations, use the tables given at the end of the book 1

Assuming that a firm pays tax at a 50 per cent rate, compute the after- tax cost of capital in the following cases: 2 2 A 8.5 per cent preference share sold at par. A perpetual bond sold at par, coupon rate of interest being 7 per cent iii) A ten-year, 8 per cent, ₹1000 par bond sold at ₹950 less 4 per cent underwriting commission. An ordinary share selling at a current market price of ₹120, and paying a current dividend of`₹9 per share, which is expected to grow at a rate of 8 per cent. An ordinary share of a company, which engages no external financing, is selling for ₹50. The earnings per share are ₹7.50 of which sixty percent is paid in dividends. The company reinvests retained earnings at a rate of 10 per cent
Describe the traditional view on the optimum capital structure. Compare and contrast this view with the NOI approach and the NI approach. 2
What are the assumptions which underlie Gordon’s model of dividend effect? Does dividend policy affect the value of the firm under Gordon’s model? Explain with example 3

Equipment A has a cost of ₹75,000 and net cash flow of ₹20,000 per year, for six years. A substitute equipment B would cost ₹50,000 and generate net cash flow of ₹14,000 per year for six years. The required rate of return of both equipments is 11 per cent. Calculate the IRR and NPV for the equipments. Which equipment should be accepted and why? 3
Briefly explain factors that determine the working capital needs of a firm 4

Explain the three principal motives for holding cash A manufacturing company has an expected usage of 50,000 units of certain product during the next year. The cost of processing an order is ₹20 and the carrying cost per unit is ₹0.50 for one year. Lead time on an order is five days and the company will keep a reserve supply of two days’ usage. You are required to calculate the economic order quantity and the reorder point. . 4
